title: The Battle for Donetsk
gist: Podcast episode notes (members-only teaser) for The Russia Contingency: frontline reporter Francis Farrell describes the fight for the Donetsk fortress belt around Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Kramatorsk, and Dobropillia, and whether a recent shift in momentum marks a real turning point in the Russo-Ukrainian war.

insights.claim: The battle for the Donetsk fortress belt is unfolding across Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Kramatorsk, and Dobropillia, with Russian infiltrations having overrun a city.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Identifies the specific eastern Ukraine axis where the front is most active, relevant to tracking war trajectory.
insights.quote: how the battle has been unfolding across Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Kramatorsk, and Dobropillia.

insights.claim: The episode raises whether a recent shift in momentum on the eastern front marks a real turning point in the Russo-Ukrainian war.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Flags an open question on whether the war's momentum is durably shifting, a key macro/geopolitical variable.
insights.quote: whether the recent shift in momentum marks a real turning point in the war.
